paper_focus.title: Behavior of Fe-Mn-Al-C Steels during Cyclic Tests
paper_focus.research_objective: To elucidate the behavior of Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic steels under cyclic loading, including elasto-plastic deformation and cyclic temperatures.
material_systems: Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic steel (nominal base composition ~29 wt% Mn, ~9 wt% Al, ~0.9 wt% C)
Martensitic chromium steel Fe-12Cr-1.5Ni-0.2V-1.8W-0.5Mo-0.15C (for thermal fatigue comparison)
Martensitic chromium steel Fe-13Cr-0.2C (for elasto-plastic bending comparison)
main_process_variables: Solution treatment: water quenched from 1323 K
Fatigue test: rotating bending (Schenk machine), baseline 2 × 10^7 cycles
Fatigue test temperatures: 293 K, 673 K, thermocycling 293 K ↔ 673 K (heating to 673 K + pulse cooling in distilled water to 293 K)
Thermal fatigue test: restricted thermal expansion (80% restriction), contact electric resistance heating to 873 K, compressed air cooling to 313 K, cycles 873 K ↔ 313 K
Elasto-plastic bending test: constant deflection 150 mm, specimens φ 50 mm gauge
Tensile test: 5 mm diameter, 25 mm gauge length
Metallographic etching: 4–7% nitric acid in ethanol
TEM foil preparation: electropolishing in chromic acid (60 g CrO3) + phosphoric acid (400 mL H3PO4), 20–50 °C, 15–20 V, 5–8 A/cm²
